title: Vertical destination family phase
summary: The upper old-growth family now has a real remembered destination. The next lane-3 gain should answer it from below with one tucked under-basin place inside the existing cave family, not another depth spike or cue layer.

execution_order.why_now: The next lane-3 gain is one place the player remembers, not another tiny spatial repair.

execution_order.goal: Implement a forest vertical destination family.
execution_order.why_now: A stronger upper-canopy or giant-tree destination can turn the current proof points into a real chapter-space.

execution_order.goal: Review the forest vertical destination family.
execution_order.why_now: The destination should add wonder and memory without getting harsher or noisier.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the deeper cave pocket handoff.
execution_order.why_now: Once the upper destination is stronger, the cave side should answer it with one calmer remembered place below the basin lip.

execution_order.goal: Implement a deeper cave pocket follow-on.
execution_order.why_now: A second tied destination will make the forest vertical family feel like one real place rather than separate tricks.

execution_order.goal: Review the deeper cave pocket follow-on.
execution_order.why_now: The cave beat should stay cozy, recoverable, and readable.

avoid: floor or worldHeight deepening below the current under-basin pocket
new chamber or right-side tunnel under log-run
second climb spine or second upper-return nook
new vertical cues or HUD work
new species pack, journal shell, or station drift

guardrails: Reuse the current terrain, platform, climbable, and cue systems.
Do not add a traversal HUD or harsher punishment language.
Favor remembered habitat pockets over raw height or depth.
Keep every new beat recoverable and kid-readable.
For main-214, strengthen the inward old-growth canopy pocket around crown-window and inner-rest instead of adding more height or another outward top route.
For main-215, strengthen the under-basin chamber as one tucked resting place below stone-basin instead of adding a new right-side tunnel, deeper floor, or second climb spine.
